summary: PR 1093 committed the real, expanded `config/GALE01/splits.todo.txt`, adding thousands of split entries and section address ranges for GALE01. The change appears to repair or complete configuration data referenced from PR #1092, turning `splits.todo.txt` into a broad map of unresolved or planned source-file splits across lb, cm, pl, mp, ef, ft, and many ftCommon action files. No review discussion was present in the provided slice.
what_changed: Replaced a much smaller `splits.todo.txt` with a large committed version: 4066 added lines and 22 deleted lines in one config file.
Added many source-file split records with `.text`, `.rodata`, `.data`, `.bss`, `.sdata`, `.sbss`, and `.sdata2` start/end address ranges.
Adjusted `melee/lb/lbcollision.c` to include an `.sdata` range from `0x804D36A0` to `0x804D3710`.

Split the previous `melee/lb/lbrefract2.c` range: its `.text` now ends at `0x80022C30`, `.sdata2` now ends at `0x804D7DB0`, and a new `melee/lb/lbrefract3.c` entry owns `0x80022C30-0x8002305C`, `.rodata` `0x803B7300-0x803B73B8`, and `.sdata2` `0x804D7DB0-0x804D7DD8`.

Added broad split coverage for early DOL systems including `melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.c`, `melee/cm/camera.c`, `melee/cm/cmsnap.c`, `melee/pl/player.c`, `melee/mp/mpcoll.c`, `melee/mp/mplib.c`, and effect files such as `melee/ef/eflib.c`, `melee/ef/efsync.c`, `melee/ef/ef_061D.c`, and `melee/ef/efasync.c`.

Added many fighter and common-action split records, including `melee/ft/fighter.c`, `ftanim.c`, `ftaction.c`, `ftparts.c`, `ftcamera.c`, `ftcoll.c`, `ftcommon.c`, `ftdrawcommon.c`, `ftdata.c`, `ftlib.c`, and many `melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_*` files.
smart_moves: Kept the change isolated to `config/GALE01/splits.todo.txt`, making this a pure split-map/config correction rather than mixing it with code or symbol changes.
Recorded per-section ranges, not just `.text`, which is useful for future matching work involving data, small data, BSS, rodata, and small rodata ownership.
Created a separate `lbrefract3.c` entry instead of leaving its ranges folded into `lbrefract2.c`, preserving more granular file boundaries for future decompilation.
Used source-path-oriented entries such as `melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_AttackAir.c`, which makes the todo split map directly searchable by expected decomp file location.
decomp_lessons: For large decomp projects, committing an accurate `splits.todo.txt` matters because it gives contributors a shared address-range map for files that are not yet fully split or implemented.
When correcting split ownership, update all affected sections together; the `lbrefract2.c` to `lbrefract3.c` adjustment shows `.text`, `.rodata`, and `.sdata2` boundaries moving consistently.
Config-only split updates can be valuable infrastructure PRs even when they do not add matched C code, because they reduce ambiguity for later module extraction and matching.
Address ranges in split configuration should preserve contiguous boundaries between neighboring files, e.g. one file ending at the exact address where the next begins.
naming_conventions: Split entries use repository source paths ending in `.c`, such as `melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.c` and `melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Attack1.c`.
Unknown or address-derived files use hexadecimal-style suffixes, including names like `ft_07C1.c`, `ft_081B.c`, `ftCo_08A6.c`, and `ftCo_09CB.c`.
Common fighter action files follow the `ftCo_` prefix with CamelCase action names, such as `ftCo_AttackDash.c`, `ftCo_DamageFall.c`, `ftCo_ShieldBreakStand.c`, and `ftCo_CliffWait.c`.
Section labels in `splits.todo.txt` are lowercase DOL section names with leading dots: `.text`, `.rodata`, `.data`, `.bss`, `.sdata`, `.sbss`, and `.sdata2`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `splits.todo.txt` as an address ownership guide when deciding where unmatched functions and data should live.
Track small-data sections explicitly; the diff adds or preserves `.sdata`, `.sbss`, and `.sdata2` ranges for many files, which helps avoid mismatches from misplaced globals or constants.
When splitting a file, make the old file's end address and the new file's start address identical for each relevant section to maintain linker-contiguous boundaries.

Include data-only files when needed, as shown by entries such as `melee/ft/ftdrawcommon_data.c`, `melee/ft/ftdata2.c`, and `melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Init.c` with data or small-data ranges but no `.text` in the excerpt.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.

The PR body only says: `See https://github.com/doldecomp/melee/pull/1092#issue-2095769023.` The reason for the missing or incorrect prior commit is implied by the title but not explained in this slice.
follow_up_queries: Inspect PR #1092 issue/comment context to understand why `splits.todo.txt` needed to be committed again or 'for real'.
Search later PRs touching `config/GALE01/splits.todo.txt` to see whether these address ranges were consumed by dtk/split config generation or later file extraction.
Look for later decomp PRs adding source files named in this todo list, especially `lbrefract3.c`, `lbaudio_ax.c`, and `ftCo_*` files, to trace how this split map guided matching work.
